reviews.author: Bảo Quang Nguyễn
reviews.text: Gành Đá Đĩa (Reef of Stone Plates), a masterpiece of nature gifted to my hometown, Phú Yên ❤ Tucked away in a serene coastal area about 35 km from Tuy Hoa City, Đá Đĩa Reef rises majestically and beautifully like a giant beehive amid the land, sky, and sea. This remarkable site is a natural wonder formed by volcanic lava. When the volcano became inactive, molten lava flowed out and cooled rapidly upon contact with seawater, creating rock formations that resemble neatly stacked plates. That’s why the place is known as “Gành Đá Đĩa” (Reef of Stone Plates). Its unique structure is extremely rare, with only four or five similar sites in the world, making it a major attraction for visitors. Every visit to Đá Đĩa Reef leaves me feeling overwhelmed by the power and beauty of nature. I love the pure, fresh air; the sea breeze mixed with the scent of seawater and sunlight creates a perfect harmony gifted by Mother Nature to my hometown. If you have a chance to visit Phú Yên, this famous site is truly a must-see destination. From here, you can also explore nearby attractions such as Gành Đèn Lighthouse, Mằng Lăng Ancient Church, or Ô Loan Lagoon. As a local resident who grew up in Phú Yên, I wholeheartedly recommend this place, along with many other beautiful sites in my hometown. ❤

reviews.author: GOONER.
reviews.text: Located in An Ninh Dong Commune, Phu Yen Province, Gành Đá Đĩa (The Cliff of Stone Plates) is a breathtaking geological wonder that seems almost too perfect to be natural. Formed by volcanic eruptions millions of years ago, the cooling lava created thousands of basalt columns in hexagonal and pentagonal shapes. From a distance, the entire site resembles a giant honeycomb or a stack of black stone plates neatly arranged by a divine hand, stretching out towards the turquoise East Sea. ​What makes Gành Đá Đĩa truly captivating is the harmony between the rugged, dark stones and the white sea foam crashing against them. Walking on these ancient rocks, you can feel the immense power of time and nature. The best time to visit is at sunrise or sunset, when the golden light reflects off the basalt surfaces, creating a shimmering, mystical landscape. Unlike the more famous Giant’s Causeway in Ireland, Gành Đá Đĩa feels more intimate and untouched. It is not just a destination for photography; it is a place to sit quietly, breathe in the salty air, and marvel at one of the most unique coastal landscapes in the world.❤🇻🇳🧡

reviews.author: Jonatan Jonsson
reviews.text: Very unique geological experience if you are interested in nature! Cool stone formations, which are connected to a small tourist village for with food and souvenirs. It is a bit off any big city, so we went there with a Grab which stayed during the 1,5h we were there, for the return trip.

reviews.author: Anna Baker
reviews.text: A beautiful place to see with interesting basalt rock formations. Walk the loop at the top as great views of the sea. Arrive before 10am as the tour buses arrive then with people who trample all over the rocks trying to get the best photo of themselves! It was super quiet before then and lovely to see. You can also walk to light house- 10mins walk.

reviews.author: Gia Minh Lương
reviews.text: this beach is absolutely stunning! The water is crystal clear with beautiful shades of turquoise and deep blue. The coastline is peaceful, and the waves gently crash against the black rocks, creating a relaxing sound. It’s not crowded, so it feels very calm and natural. The view of the green hills in the background makes it even more picturesque. Perfect place to unwind, take photos, or just enjoy the sea breeze

reviews.author: Steve Vuu
reviews.text: Beautiful place for a quick visit when you are in Phú Yên. Unfortunately there were so much trash, rubbish around, later we realised it must be due to the recent typhoon and flood few months back. Though local gov should organise clean up and remind visitors not to litter in the area. And if you can, visit during a more quiet time as it was Tet, so the place was jam packed.

reviews.author: Hunor Gocz
reviews.text: Really beautiful place. It is worth visiting if you have the time for it. There is no public transportation to this place so you need to hire a driver or join a tour which visits this place. We were lucky as we managed to visit this place when it was empty. We had it all for ourselves.

reviews.author: FrancisP
reviews.text: The natural scenery here is very beautiful for taking pictures. However, this place is extremely crowded during holidays. The weather in the area is very hot and you need to walk a long distance to get to the place to visit, so it will be quite hot. However, the shops here can lend you umbrellas to walk. It will be difficult for you to take beautiful pictures during holidays because there are so many visitors. Tickets are sold at the gate, children are free. There are many food and souvenir shops inside and outside the tourist area. A large parking lot is right outside the gate, but there is still a lack of parking during holidays.

reviews.author: Matthias Kreier
reviews.text: There are only a few places in the world with these hexagonal pyroclastic columns, and this spot in Vietnam is one of them! This unique feature of nature can be easy and fast explored by food. Some extra touristic stuff is added (all kinds of souvenirs) and busses for local tourists arrive. Near the entrance you can listen to traditional music.

reviews.author: Bruce Nguyen
reviews.text: So awesome, I love all the view, every angle. The little thing make me concern is parking fee, as image you see, only 2k for bike but the security ask me for 5k, I wonder why and he tell me that was the old board (2 or 5 just small amount but i didn't feel the honest in him, this board look new not old by the way). But totally this is good experience and really recommended.

reviews.author: Lily Nguyen
reviews.text: Geology here is unique, it’s such a wonderful place to see pentagon rock tube aligning together. There is stone music in a nice local coffee. They have nice yogurt with jackfruit and Dac nut The ticket is a bit over price for experience here (40k for adult, children are free) Not much to do, I prefer Co Thach mountain near by much more

reviews.author: Nguyen Tuan Khoi
reviews.text: Natural rock formation that becomes a tourist attraction, with a walking track passing it. Entry fee is charged per person

reviews.author: Ravinder K
reviews.text: My visit to Sea Cliff of Stone Plates was exceptionally pleasant. The natural formations were captivating, presenting a truly remarkable geological display. I found the overall experience to be highly rewarding. ⛰️🌊🌟✨

reviews.author: Arno H
reviews.text: Pretty good. You cannot see volcano rocks like that in many places which convinced me to do 2h of motorbike. The road from Tuy Hòais good on half the way then not to good when you arrive towards the tourist area. There was some traditional singing for the new year week

reviews.author: PL Lau
reviews.text: A Natural Marvel with a Crowd Gành Đá Đĩa is a stunning natural wonder with its unique hexagonal basalt rock formations. The views are absolutely breathtaking, especially when the sun hits the rocks and creates a mesmerizing play of light and shadow. The unique hexagonal rock formations, formed from solidified lava flows, create a breathtaking landscape unlike any other. The sheer scale and intricate patterns of the rocks are a testament to the power of nature. However, be prepared for crowds, as this is a popular tourist spot. It can be a bit challenging to find a quiet spot to fully appreciate the beauty, but it's definitely worth the effort. => What to bring: Comfortable shoes, sunscreen, a hat, and plenty of water.

reviews.author: Franklin Franco (donffrank)
reviews.text: Very quiet, beautiful and peaceful spot, you have to pay to enter, in the parking lot upon arrival they will lend you an umbrella for free (you can tip after returning it). It's a very small walk,there's a gift shop, ice cream, sugar cane juice, and other refreshing options... Then you follow a small path and you will arrive at the bluestones. It's peaceful, there's one area where all the people gather to take pictures, and another area where you can get your feet wet. Also very clean! Not too much litter compared with other tourist spots.

reviews.author: i3 r
reviews.text: Really unique view, if you stay in tuy Hoa, get a scooter and drive here (50min, but cool views on the way). Really cool view for pictures and not too many people. 40k per person, parking 5k, and maybe 20-40k for some bracelets (some kids will come to you if you park bike so you can support them if you can)

reviews.author: braden
reviews.text: Very cool place. Don’t miss this on your drive through vietnam. Many foreign tourists don’t get to see it so it’s special to be able to make this stop. Also the light house near by was very cool too (maybe even better)

reviews.author: Daria Kurilenko
reviews.text: Da Dia Reef is a true natural wonder! The basalt rocks look like they were carefully arranged by hand — absolutely stunning and mesmerizing. A must-see with your own eyes

reviews.author: Phúc Phạm
reviews.text: These stones with special shapes are great for travelers to explore. They are definitely a natural wonder. You need to buy a ticket for about 40,000 VND to enter and enjoy

reviews.author: Annie Le
reviews.text: A place with stunning views. Prepare to walk through the stairs, and climb the rocks. Sometimes the place is full of people but they clear out pretty fast. I recommend going here around 4-6PM or very early morning like 5AM because the rest of the time it’s gonna be burning hot. There is no shade to cover you from the sun, and the rocks are gonna be really hot during the day. If you go later in the afternoon, the golden hour is perfect for photos but the light will go away quite fast

reviews.author: Andrea Seminatore
reviews.text: Craw and tourist place , overrated, you have to pay a little ticket to enter. It's a very small walk,there's a gift shop, ice cream, sugar cane juice, and other refreshing options...

reviews.author: Hoa Dân Lâm
reviews.text: A truly unique and breathtaking natural wonder. The hexagonal stone columns look like something from another planet. It's peaceful, scenic, and perfect for taking photos - especially during sunrise or sunset.

reviews.author: Nguyen Hanh
reviews.text: Management board of this tourist attraction don’t have strong rule for tourist to keep that place being clean. I saw a lot of plastic garbage over there. I have visited this place few years ago without charge, now they do but it’s been worse more. Go green, keep the Earth clean

reviews.author: Thao Nguyen
reviews.text: Interesting place to see how the lava from volcano can form such a masterpiece on the ocean. Once-in-a-lifetime destination that you should check it out. The road to this place has some bumpy parts because they are still being constructed. However, still a smooth ride. You should go and come back before sunset as the roads don’t have streetlights.

reviews.author: Luân Phan
reviews.text: Beautiful view with special volcano rocks all over the place. The sun is really strong so i recommend an umbrella while strolling along the path. Not recommended for high heels. The minus point here is the most special spire is wrapped with signs, making it difficult to take good pics.

reviews.author: PIERRE PHU TIEN NGUYEN
reviews.text: One needs to visit this place to admire the forces of mother nature.

reviews.author: Linh Tran
reviews.text: 20 years ago, no business at this place, no restriction. But now too many tourism, too many people, restriction and block everywhere. It become a good place for view but also change to commercial business.

reviews.author: Phương (Capture Charm)
reviews.text: When you visit Phu Yen, there's one place you absolutely cannot miss – Ganh Da Dia. This natural wonder is a photographer's dream, with its unique rock formations that resemble stacked plates. Located in the province of Phu Yen, Ganh Da Dia offers a breathtaking landscape that showcases the beauty of Mother Nature. Upon arrival at Ganh Da Dia, you'll find yourself in a well-organized tourist area. Visitors are provided with complimentary hats to shield them from the sun. The site also offers a wide range of amenities, including coffee stalls, snack vendors, and souvenir shops, ensuring you have everything you need for a comfortable visit. To gain entry, tickets can be purchased at the gate for a reasonable price of 20,000 VND per person. While Ganh Da Dia provides electric cart services to transport visitors from the entrance to the rocky formations, many travelers opt to take a leisurely stroll. The short walk from the entrance to the rock formations allows you to soak in the natural beauty of the area at your own pace. To reach Ganh Da Dia from Tuy Hoa City, you can use your own means of transportation and head north along the coastline for approximately 37 kilometers. Along the way, you'll pass other scenic spots such as Hon Yen and Bai Xep, making it an excellent opportunity to explore more of Phu Yen's coastal beauty. Ganh Da Dia is a testament to the stunning geological formations found in Vietnam. Its unique appearance is a natural wonder that will leave you in awe. Whether you're a photographer looking for the perfect shot or simply a nature enthusiast, Ganh Da Dia is an absolute must-visit destination when exploring the beauty of Phu Yen. So, make sure to include this mesmerizing site in your travel itinerary and create unforgettable memories in this picturesque corner of Vietnam.

reviews.author: Kris Gillespie
reviews.text: If you like rocks, and you work in a warehouse, you’ll love this. It’s like stacked rocks. I mean, come on.
